summary: Agentic decision layer for connected-TV planning, activation, and measurement.
editor_note: The bet is that CTV buying broke at the decision layer, not the media pipes. Olyzon maps inventory, scores programs, adapts creative, activates across DSPs and publishers, and claims +20% brand lift plus 15-30% mid-funnel improvement. Unlike panel-style measurement vendors, it tries to turn context, reach, and proof into one operating loop. The risk is whether agencies let an agentic layer sit above their existing stack.

product_evidence.claim: The official site describes an agentic layer connected to DSPs, SSPs, and direct publishers that maps inventory, scores programs, activates plans, and proves outcomes.
Olyzon presents context transparency, deduplicated reach, outcome accountability, AdCP compatibility, and impact metrics including +20% brand lift and +15-30% mid-funnel improvement.

market_context.primary_user: Agencies, brands, and trading desks buying connected-TV media across fragmented inventory sources.
market_context.differentiation: Agentic decisioning layer above existing CTV pipes instead of a single measurement, DSP, or inventory product.
market_context.why_now: The May 2026 Series A led by S4S Ventures supports US and EMEA expansion as CTV buying shifts from manual planning into AI-assisted workflows.

risks.claim: The open question is whether agencies allow an agentic layer to sit above existing planning, activation, and measurement stacks.
risks.basis: VentureDex editorial assessment based on official product evidence and funding-source review.
